commit.message:
fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: align agent text with deal cards via [DEALS: id,...] tag

GPT now appends a [DEALS: id1,id2] tag listing exactly the deal IDs
it describes. Edge function parses this, filters sourced deals to only
those IDs, and strips the tag before returning the visible text.
Removes the hardcoded .slice(0,3) cap from the frontend.

재무 계획 리팩토링: 3인 린 팀 기준, 소수정예 운영 원칙 반영

- 비용 구조: 인건비 Year1 1억(3인) → Year2 2.5억(5인) → Year3 5억(10인)
- 손익: Year1 BEP 근접, Year2부터 흑자 (10.3억)
- 투자: 부트스트랩 → Pre-Seed 1~2억 → Seed 5~10억 → Series A 30~50억
- 팀 운영: 최대 30명 이내, AI 자동화로 효율 극대화
- KPI Phase 2: 체크리스트 완료율 → 구독 전환율 5%
- 제품 기획: [전문가 & 쇼핑] 탭 추가 (전문가 목록 + 자재 쇼핑 통합)

commit.message:
Release v4.4.1: Tool restructure, admin tool, schema generation, and CLI setup

Combines v4.3.0 through v4.4.1:
- v4.3.0: CLI setup command and gen-context freshness checks
- v4.3.1: Windows path handling, NaN date fixes, test coverage
- v4.3.2: ARCHITECTURE.md and README.md documentation rewrites
- v4.4.0: New admin tool, Zod schema generation, auto-type inference
- v4.4.1: ARCHITECTURE.md handler map and tools section fixes

Key changes:
- New `admin` tool (18 actions) for system maintenance and observability
- Goal/remember actions folded into memory tool with backward-compat redirects
- Tool count reduced from 7 to 6
- Schema generation from Zod eliminates duplicate definitions
- New structural drift-prevention tests
- Windows path handling fixed in CLI setup
- gen-context freshness optimization (30-minute skip window)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(e2e): F1e modal focus trap — wait for React hydration + explicit key sequence

Root cause: waitForReviewPageReady only checks h1 visibility (SSR output),
but keyboard bindings are registered via useEffect which runs AFTER hydration.
The Ctrl+Shift+/ keypress fires before bindings exist.

Fix:
- Add waitForReviewPageHydrated() that waits for finding grid rows (confirms
  React effects including keyboard binding registration have executed)
- Click body to ensure headless Chromium has keyboard focus on the page
- Use explicit keyboard.down/up sequence for cross-platform reliability
- Increase dialog visibility timeout to 10s

commit.message:
Fix CX ticket visibility mismatch and back-navigation bug

Problem 1 — 403 on ticket open (Tooba/Hamza):
filterTicketsByDepartmentAccess used isCXTicketVisible() which matched
ANY ticket with a vendorHandle, regardless of department. So CX users
saw Finance/Ops/etc tickets in their list (75 tickets from 4 depts)
but canViewTicket gates by ticket.department === 'CX', causing 403
when they clicked any non-CX ticket.

Fix: filterTicketsByDepartmentAccess for CX now uses
ticket.department === 'CX' — consistent with canViewTicket.
CX users see only CX tickets (+ tickets assigned to them).

Problem 2 — Back button always goes to All Tickets:
When a ticket returned 403/404, the 'Ticket not found' screen had a
hardcoded setLocation('/tickets') button, ignoring the ?from= param.
So Tooba opening a ticket from My Tickets and hitting 403 would
land on All Tickets instead of returning to My Tickets.

Fix: 'Back to Tickets' now calls getBackPath() which respects the
?from=my-tickets / ?from=all-tickets query parameter.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add useEffect ref syncs to prevent stale refs in keyboard handler

Root cause: 30+ places call setClips/setCurrentTime/setSelectedClipIds
directly (not through synced wrappers), causing clipsRef, currentTimeRef,
and selectedClipIdsRef to become stale. When Q/W/M shortcuts read these
refs, they see old values and can't find clips at the current position.

Fix: add useEffect syncs for clips, currentTime, selectedClipIds so refs
stay up-to-date regardless of which code path mutates the state.

Also adds debug console.log for Q/W/M shortcuts to verify handler fires
and show actual ref values (check browser DevTools Console).

commit.message:
fix: Manas classification was 100% broken — case mismatch with MahaBuddhi VM

MahaBuddhi.think() returns lowercase trinity names (carrier, deliverer)
and lowercase approaches (karma, genesis, moksha, dharma). Manas mapped
against uppercase (BRAHMA, KARMA, MOKSHA) — every lookup missed, falling
through to guna defaults. All tasks classified as RESEARCH/FLASH.

Found by actually booting steward and observing its behavior live.
